objects.name: PAP
objects.description: This defines PAP as a STIX extension

objects.name: Operation Dindoor
objects.description: Targeting of U.S. banks, airports, and software companies supplying the defense industry, as well as Canadian NGOs. The group uses a new Deno-based backdoor named 'Dindoor'.

objects.content:
Sector: Finance, Aerospace, Defense, Transportation, NGO

Modus operandi:
Use of custom Python and Deno-based backdoors (Dindoor, Fakeset). Deployment via Backblaze storage. Data exfiltration using Rclone to Wasabi cloud storage.

objects.name: UAT-9244 Telco Campaign
objects.description: China-linked threat actor targeting telecommunication service providers in South America using a multi-stage malware toolkit.

objects.content:
Sector: Telecommunications

Modus operandi:
DLL side-loading (wsprint.exe), peer-to-peer C2 communication via BitTorrent protocol (PeerTime backdoor), and converting edge devices into mass-scanning proxy nodes (BruteEntry).

objects.name: Operation BadPaw
objects.description: Espionage campaign targeting Ukrainian organizations with multi-stage infection chains using decoy documents related to border crossing appeals.

objects.content:
Sector: Government, Defense

Modus operandi:
Phishing via ukr.net, HTA files with registry-based environmental checks (sandbox evasion), steganography in images to hide payloads, and deployment of BadPaw loader and MeowMeow backdoor.

objects.name: CVE-2026-29000
objects.description:
Maximum severity authentication bypass in the pac4j-jwt library. Flaw in JWE processing allows remote users to forge tokens without a valid signature using only the server's RSA public key.

Affected product: pac4j-jwt

Affected vendor: pac4j

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:pac4j:pac4j-jwt:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: CVE-2026-1492
objects.description:
Critical flaw in WordPress plugin allowing unauthenticated creation of administrator accounts by supplying user-defined roles during registration.

Affected product: User Registration & Membership plugin

Affected vendor: WPEverest

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:wpeverest:user_registration:*:*:*:*:*:wordpress:*:*

objects.name: CVE-2026-25611
objects.description:
High-severity DoS vulnerability in the wire protocol compression (OP_COMPRESSED). Allows unauthenticated attackers to trigger an Out-of-Memory (OOM) crash with minimal bandwidth.

Affected product: MongoDB Server

Affected vendor: MongoDB

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:mongodb:mongodb:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: CVE-2026-20122
objects.description:
Arbitrary file overwrite vulnerability being actively exploited in the wild. Requires valid read-only credentials with API access.

Affected product: Catalyst SD-WAN Manager

Affected vendor: Cisco

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:cisco:catalyst_sd-wan_manager:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: Breach: Wikipedia (Meta-Wiki)
objects.description:
A self-propagating JavaScript worm vandalized Meta-Wiki pages and modified approximately 85 users' common.js files to achieve persistence. The worm was triggered during a security review of user-authored code.

Sector: Technology, Media

objects.name: Breach: U.S. Marshals Service
objects.description:
Theft of $46 million in cryptocurrency from government-seized assets (including Bitfinex hack funds) by John Daghita, the son of a contractor CEO.

Sector: Government

objects.name: Breach: France Ministry of Health
objects.description:
Data breach exposing administrative information for 15.8 million patients and sensitive doctors' notes for 165,000 individuals.

Sector: Health

objects.content:
Sector: Global

Theme: US-Israel-Iran Armed Conflict

Following joint military strikes on February 28, cyber operations have escalated. Iran faces a near-total internet blackout (1% traffic). Pro-Iranian groups like DieNet are targeting Gulf state infrastructure (Bahrain, UAE, Jordan) in retaliation.

Tags: Iran, Israel, USA, Geopolitics, Internet Blackout

objects.name: Industrialized Brute-Force against FortiGate using Generative AI
objects.description:
Attackers are using commercial GenAI services to automate the exploitation of exposed FortiGate management interfaces with weak passwords. Over 600 devices in 55 countries compromised.

Analysis:
The use of GenAI lowers the technical barrier for attackers, allowing for massive scaling of simple attacks. Compromised configurations are used to pivot into internal networks toward Active Directory.

Recommendations:
- Disable administrative interface exposure to the public internet.
- Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all administrative access.

objects.name: Malicious OpenClaw AI Agents Campaign
objects.description:
Poisoned Bing AI search results promote fake OpenClaw GitHub repositories. Malicious 'skills' install info-stealers (Vidar) and proxy malware.

Analysis:
Threat actors are exploiting the popularity of autonomous agents to gain high-privilege system access. Prompt injection is also reported as a delivery vector.

Recommendations:
- Only install OpenClaw and its skills from the official validated repository.
- Implement method-scoped approvals (ClawGuard) to limit agent permissions.

objects.content: The cyber threat landscape as of March 6, 2026, is characterized by a significant convergence of kinetic warfare and industrialized cyber operations. The US-Israel-Iran conflict has reached a critical point with 'Operation Epic Fury,' accompanied by nationwide internet blackouts in Iran and targeted cyber-physical reconnaissance against IP camera infrastructure. Simultaneously, the 'SaaSpocalypse' trend highlights growing enterprise anxiety over AI-native agents, while threat actors are already leveraging Generative AI to automate brute-force attacks (e.g., against FortiGate) and develop sophisticated malware. Data from 2025 retrospectives indicates a 30% increase in active exploitation of known vulnerabilities (KEV), with a structural shift toward targeting enterprise edge devices and security appliances.
